tool_calls.name: submit_final_result
tool_calls.args:
{"final_result": "## Implied Monthly Starlink ARPU Estimate — Q1 2026

### Data Inputs from the S-1 Filing

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Q1 2026 Connectivity Segment Revenue | $3,257 million |
| — Consumer Revenue | $2,148 million |
| — Enterprise & Government Revenue | $1,109 million |
| — Products (Hardware) Revenue | $380 million |
| — Services Revenue (implied) | $2,877 million |
| Starlink Subscribers, Dec 31, 2025 | 8.9 million |
| Starlink Subscribers, Mar 31, 2026 | 10.3 million |
| Average Subscribers (simple avg.) | 9.6 million |

---

### ARPU Estimates at Different Revenue Perimeters

**1. Total Connectivity Revenue ÷ Average Subscribers (Broadest Measure)**

$$\frac{\$3{,}257\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3\text{ months}} = \$113.09 \text{/month}$$

This is the simplest calculation but significantly overstates per-subscriber revenue because the numerator includes revenue streams not attributable to the subscriber denominator.

**2. Connectivity Services Revenue Only (Excludes Hardware)**

$$\frac{\$2{,}877\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3} = \$99.90 \text{/month}$$

Removes one-time Starlink Kit/terminal sales ($380M) but still includes Enterprise & Government service revenue.

**3. Consumer Revenue Only (Excludes Enterprise & Government)**

$$\frac{\$2{,}148\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3} = \$74.58 \text{/month}$$

Removes Enterprise & Government revenue ($1,109M) but still includes consumer hardware sales within the numerator.

**4. Filing's Own Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU**

The S-1 discloses a **Starlink Subscriber ARPU of $66/month** for Q1 2026. This is calculated by the company as:

$$\text{ARPU} = \frac{\text{Service Revenue from Starlink Subscribers}}{\text{Average Starlink Subscribers} \times \text{Months}}$$

Back-solving: $66 × 9.6M × 3 = **~$1,901 million** in service revenue from Starlink Subscribers, implying ~$247M of the $380M hardware revenue is attributable to consumer subscribers and ~$133M to enterprise/government customers.

---

### Key Assumptions

1. **Average subscribers:** I used a simple average of beginning-of-quarter (8.9M at Dec 31, 2025) and end-of-quarter (10.3M at Mar 31, 2026) subscriber counts = 9.6M. The filing does not disclose daily or monthly average subscriber counts. Subscriber growth was rapid (~105% YoY), so the true quarterly average could differ from the simple midpoint if growth was non-linear within the quarter.

2. **Revenue attribution:** The filing does not provide a granular breakdown of how much service revenue is generated specifically from "Starlink Subscribers" (as defined) versus enterprise/government customers. The $66 ARPU figure is the company's own calculation using its internal subscriber-level revenue attribution.

3. **Three-month quarter:** Q1 2026 = January, February, March = 3 months.

4. **Recast financials:** All figures reflect the retrospectively recast consolidated entity (including xAI merged Feb 2, 2026 and X Holdings merged Mar 28, 2025). However, xAI is reported in the separate AI segment, so Connectivity segment figures are unaffected by the xAI merger.

---

### What the Filing's Subscriber and Revenue Definitions Exclude from ARPU

**Excluded from the Subscriber Count (Denominator):**
- **Enterprise & Government customers** with direct negotiated contracts — including aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed-site, and government/Starshield entities. These customers generate $1,109M in Q1 2026 revenue but are not counted as "Starlink Subscribers."
- **Starlink Mobile (MNO partnership) end-users** — the ~7.4 million monthly unique devices receiving satellite-to-mobile services are not counted as subscribers; their revenue is classified under Enterprise & Government.
- **Multiple end-users per Service Line** — the filing acknowledges the actual number of individual humans using Starlink is "meaningfully higher" than 10.3M because households and businesses share single Service Lines.

**Excluded from the ARPU Revenue (Numerator):**
- **Hardware/Products revenue** ($380M in Q1 2026) — the ARPU formula uses only *service* revenue, excluding one-time Starlink Kit terminal sales.
- **Enterprise & Government service revenue** — all revenue from negotiated enterprise/government contracts (aviation, maritime, Starshield, MNO partnerships) is excluded from the ARPU numerator because these customers are not "Starlink Subscribers."
- **xAI / AI segment revenue** — reported in a separate segment and not part of Connectivity at all.

**Net Effect:** The filing's $66 ARPU captures only the recurring service revenue from standard consumer and small-business subscription plans (Residential, Roam, Local Priority, Global Priority). The gap between the broadest estimate ($113.09) and the filing's ARPU ($66) is driven primarily by the $1,109M in Enterprise & Government revenue and $380M in hardware revenue that are included in total Connectivity revenue but excluded from the ARPU calculation.

---

### ARPU Trend Context

| Period | Disclosed ARPU |
|---|---|
| FY 2023 | $99 |
| FY 2024 | $91 |
| Q1 2025 | $86 |
| FY 2025 | $81 |
| Q1 2026 | $66 |

The 22.9% YoY decline (Q1 2025 → Q1 2026) is attributed to international expansion into lower-income markets, introduction of lower-priced plans, and fee adjustments. Management expects ARPU to continue declining as the subscriber base shifts toward emerging markets.

---

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm",
      "name": "SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement (Filed May 20, 2026)"
    }
  ]
}"}

parsed_args.final_result:
## Implied Monthly Starlink ARPU Estimate — Q1 2026

### Data Inputs from the S-1 Filing

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Q1 2026 Connectivity Segment Revenue | $3,257 million |
| — Consumer Revenue | $2,148 million |
| — Enterprise & Government Revenue | $1,109 million |
| — Products (Hardware) Revenue | $380 million |
| — Services Revenue (implied) | $2,877 million |
| Starlink Subscribers, Dec 31, 2025 | 8.9 million |
| Starlink Subscribers, Mar 31, 2026 | 10.3 million |
| Average Subscribers (simple avg.) | 9.6 million |

---

### ARPU Estimates at Different Revenue Perimeters

**1. Total Connectivity Revenue ÷ Average Subscribers (Broadest Measure)**

$$\frac{\$3{,}257\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3\text{ months}} = \$113.09 \text{/month}$$

This is the simplest calculation but significantly overstates per-subscriber revenue because the numerator includes revenue streams not attributable to the subscriber denominator.

**2. Connectivity Services Revenue Only (Excludes Hardware)**

$$\frac{\$2{,}877\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3} = \$99.90 \text{/month}$$

Removes one-time Starlink Kit/terminal sales ($380M) but still includes Enterprise & Government service revenue.

**3. Consumer Revenue Only (Excludes Enterprise & Government)**

$$\frac{\$2{,}148\text{M}}{9.6\text{M} \times 3} = \$74.58 \text{/month}$$

Removes Enterprise & Government revenue ($1,109M) but still includes consumer hardware sales within the numerator.

**4. Filing's Own Disclosed Starlink Subscriber ARPU**

The S-1 discloses a **Starlink Subscriber ARPU of $66/month** for Q1 2026. This is calculated by the company as:

$$\text{ARPU} = \frac{\text{Service Revenue from Starlink Subscribers}}{\text{Average Starlink Subscribers} \times \text{Months}}$$

Back-solving: $66 × 9.6M × 3 = **~$1,901 million** in service revenue from Starlink Subscribers, implying ~$247M of the $380M hardware revenue is attributable to consumer subscribers and ~$133M to enterprise/government customers.

---

### Key Assumptions

1. **Average subscribers:** I used a simple average of beginning-of-quarter (8.9M at Dec 31, 2025) and end-of-quarter (10.3M at Mar 31, 2026) subscriber counts = 9.6M. The filing does not disclose daily or monthly average subscriber counts. Subscriber growth was rapid (~105% YoY), so the true quarterly average could differ from the simple midpoint if growth was non-linear within the quarter.

2. **Revenue attribution:** The filing does not provide a granular breakdown of how much service revenue is generated specifically from "Starlink Subscribers" (as defined) versus enterprise/government customers. The $66 ARPU figure is the company's own calculation using its internal subscriber-level revenue attribution.

3. **Three-month quarter:** Q1 2026 = January, February, March = 3 months.

4. **Recast financials:** All figures reflect the retrospectively recast consolidated entity (including xAI merged Feb 2, 2026 and X Holdings merged Mar 28, 2025). However, xAI is reported in the separate AI segment, so Connectivity segment figures are unaffected by the xAI merger.

---

### What the Filing's Subscriber and Revenue Definitions Exclude from ARPU

**Excluded from the Subscriber Count (Denominator):**
- **Enterprise & Government customers** with direct negotiated contracts — including aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed-site, and government/Starshield entities. These customers generate $1,109M in Q1 2026 revenue but are not counted as "Starlink Subscribers."
- **Starlink Mobile (MNO partnership) end-users** — the ~7.4 million monthly unique devices receiving satellite-to-mobile services are not counted as subscribers; their revenue is classified under Enterprise & Government.
- **Multiple end-users per Service Line** — the filing acknowledges the actual number of individual humans using Starlink is "meaningfully higher" than 10.3M because households and businesses share single Service Lines.

**Excluded from the ARPU Revenue (Numerator):**
- **Hardware/Products revenue** ($380M in Q1 2026) — the ARPU formula uses only *service* revenue, excluding one-time Starlink Kit terminal sales.
- **Enterprise & Government service revenue** — all revenue from negotiated enterprise/government contracts (aviation, maritime, Starshield, MNO partnerships) is excluded from the ARPU numerator because these customers are not "Starlink Subscribers."
- **xAI / AI segment revenue** — reported in a separate segment and not part of Connectivity at all.

**Net Effect:** The filing's $66 ARPU captures only the recurring service revenue from standard consumer and small-business subscription plans (Residential, Roam, Local Priority, Global Priority). The gap between the broadest estimate ($113.09) and the filing's ARPU ($66) is driven primarily by the $1,109M in Enterprise & Government revenue and $380M in hardware revenue that are included in total Connectivity revenue but excluded from the ARPU calculation.

---

### ARPU Trend Context

| Period | Disclosed ARPU |
|---|---|
| FY 2023 | $99 |
| FY 2024 | $91 |
| Q1 2025 | $86 |
| FY 2025 | $81 |
| Q1 2026 | $66 |

The 22.9% YoY decline (Q1 2025 → Q1 2026) is attributed to international expansion into lower-income markets, introduction of lower-priced plans, and fee adjustments. Management expects ARPU to continue declining as the subscriber base shifts toward emerging markets.

---

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm",
      "name": "SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement (Filed May 20, 2026)"
    }
  ]
}
